GL-SELF.label: Self-motivated
GL-SELF.description: Lead independently identified STL as the right move for their property. Done their own research. Has come to the meeting with intent.
GL-SELF.close_approach: Validate their thinking, add specificity to their numbers, then close on process. Don't re-sell the STL concept — they already bought it. Sell Stayful over alternatives.
GL-SELF.note: GL-SELF is the baseline signal. Most entries carry this. The close is about Stayful vs alternatives, not STL vs LTL.

GL-MGMT.label: Management pain with current provider
GL-MGMT.description: Lead is currently being managed by an STL company and it's going wrong. Income underperformance, damage concealment, communication failures, pooled listings. Switching intent is high.
typical_behaviours: has looked at what peak income should be
GL-MGMT.close_approach: Acknowledge their specific pain point. Show exactly how Stayful handles that failure mode differently. Don't generalise. Don't criticise the provider by name — describe your process instead.
common_failures_cited: income significantly below what was promised
damage not reported or reported too late to claim
pooled listing identity — photos used for whole building
only 3 reviews in 5+ months of trading

GL-TIME.label: External time pressure
GL-TIME.description: External deadline creating real urgency: departure date, seasonal peak window, vacancy cost accumulating, or tax year consideration.
typical_behaviours: already knows income per week missed
GL-TIME.close_approach: Quantify the cost of delay. Calculate income per week missed. Tie the 13% offer to the deadline to create dual urgency. Move fast on follow-up.
example_applications: Entry 059 Shahid: 6-bed vacant 6 months, £1,500/month ongoing costs — every week without STL income = £375 loss
Entry 062 Kuldip: October half-term Alton Towers proximity = £1,928 net that month — signing before August captures this
Entry 044 Gareth: Keys May 29, must be live mid-June to capture summer peak
